messages.author: stachumann
messages.text: For last couple of days I was developing meshcore simulator. The idea started after post in general forum pointing out to Matthew D Green Quickly realized, there are two major flaws - it is NOT using companion / router firmware and is super slow. I've build version which simulates fully down to companion and router (it uses real code) - with idea of testing different settings, ideas and of course simulate them. The first reason behind was - is rx/tx/direct delay really a bottleneck? (No - still I don't know - for the reasons below). You are welcome to try: https://github.com/stachuman/meshcore_simv2 Where do I struggle? 1. Building test topology - at the moment, based on real, incomplete topology - I try to estimate test cases 2. Making sure - simulation is good - is the simulated radio close enough to the real radio? Simulating 20 minutes of traffic takes on my pc/server roughly 5 seconds - for 70 routers and 12 companions. If any of you would like to help - I'd really appreciate. The aim is - being able to test new features of firmware before release (edited)

Building test topology - at the moment, based on real, incomplete topology - I try to estimate test cases 2. Making sure - simulation is good - is the simulated radio close enough to the real radio? Simulating 20 minutes of traffic takes on my pc/server roughly 5 seconds - for 70 routers and 12 companions. If any of you would like to help - I'd really appreciate.
